user_prompt:
User Requirement: do a laminar dam break with obstacle simulation. user interfoam solver. The domain extent is 1x1x1. Use 32 grid points in all directions. Use density of air to be 1 kg/m3 and density of water to be 1000 kg/m3. Take the viscosity of air to be 0.0001 and viscosity of water to be 0.001. The simulation runs from t=0s to t=0.1s with a timestep of 0.001s and a write interval of 0.02.

Reference Directory Structure (similar case): <dir>directory name: .. File names in this directory: [Allclean]</dir>
<dir>directory name: 0. File names in this directory: [U.orig, alpha.water.orig, k, nut, omega, p_rgh]</dir>
<dir>directory name: system. File names in this directory: [blockMeshDict, controlDict, decomposeParDict, fvSchemes, fvSolution, setFieldsDict, topoSetDict]</dir>
<dir>directory name: constant. File names in this directory: [dynamicMeshDict, g, momentumTransport, phaseProperties, physicalProperties.air, physicalProperties.water]</dir>

There are 1 files in Directory: ,
There are 6 files in Directory: 0,
There are 7 files in Directory: system,
There are 6 files in Directory: constant

Make sure you generate all the necessary files for the user's requirements.Do not include any gmsh files like .geo etc. in the subtasks.Only include blockMesh or snappyHexMesh if the user hasnt requested for gmsh mesh or user isnt using an external uploaded custom meshPlease generate the output as structured JSON.
response: subtasks=[SubtaskModel(file_name='U.orig', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='alpha.water.orig', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='p_rgh', folder_name='0'), SubtaskModel(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='setFieldsDict', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='topoSetDict', folder_name='system'), SubtaskModel(file_name='g', folder_name='constant'), SubtaskModel(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant'), SubtaskModel(file_name='phaseProperties', folder_name='constant'), SubtaskModel(file_name='physicalProperties.air', folder_name='constant'), SubtaskModel(file_name='physicalProperties.water', folder_name='constant')]
